name: HTTP gateway
summary: Small local HTTP gateway for inbound jobs, channels, and session checks.

name: Basic Memory
summary: Obsidian-native, plain-markdown memory backed by Basic Memory. Indexes the vault into a local SQLite knowledge graph and exposes write/search/read MCP tools to Claude, Codex, and Gemini. Auto-captures a session checkpoint on SessionEnd/PreCompact.

name: Trello data source
summary: One-way Trello fetch with Trello-backed derived Tasks.

name: Slack channel
summary: Receives Slack app_mention and DM events, round-trips via the gateway's /chat.

name: Web channel
summary: Mobile-first browser chat surface. Serves a React UI on its own port (default 7083) and proxies the http-gateway for streaming replies.

name: Browser
summary: Headless Chromium substrate on port 7084. JPEG screencast, mouse/key/touch input, raw CDP, and Chromium's built-in DevTools reverse-proxied for any browser on the Tailnet.

name: Voice (Deepgram)
summary: Speech I/O on port 7085. Proxies Deepgram speech-to-text and text-to-speech, keeping the API key server-side; provides a kind:voice capability that channels consume for push-to-talk and read-aloud.

name: Artifact store
summary: Filesystem-backed artifact store. Documents, recordings, and other producer-Fitting output land here and surface in the sidebar browser.

name: Dev Env
summary: Consolidated dev surface on port 7086 — every Claude Code session is a tab with a Claude PTY + shell PTY and a live browser pane; worktree lifecycle and session status built in.

name: Screen share
summary: macOS screenshot capture — ~2fps JPEG polling viewer for phone/remote access.

name: Outpost
summary: Garrison Outpost bridge for a Tailscale-connected remote Mac. Spawn processes, read files, and manage git worktrees on the remote machine via Garrison Outpost Protocol v1.

name: Garrison Orchestrator
summary: Delegating Orchestrator — routes requests to specialist Soul sub-sessions via garrison-control MCP. Does not do work itself.

name: Monitor (default)
summary: Read-only visibility into every entity Garrison spawns — PIDs, status, ports, logs. Serves its own React UI on its own port (default 7077).

name: Model Router
summary: Orchestrator Faculty — a visible routing policy (Exceptions / Matrix / Continuations) that resolves each prompt to a role + discipline + continuation, then maps the role to a concrete runtime-target via the active Profile. Serves a Policy / Simulator view on its own port (default 7087).

name: Knowledge
summary: Knowledge Faculty (composite) — federates memory + context projections + code graph + symbol-nav behind one contract. Owns the canonical vault, emits cross-runtime projections (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md), and wires the shared MCPs (CodeGraph, Serena) into every runtime session, so switching the runtime never loses memory.

name: Improver
summary: Nightly self-improvement runner — reads telemetry, transcripts, and evidence and produces proposal diffs into an own-port review queue (default 7088). Approved proposals apply only through hosted authoring APIs. Per-rule autonomy (manual|auto) with streak-gated promotion and instant demotion.

name: Codex runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty (secondary) — drives `codex exec` behind the uniform RuntimeAdapter + runtime-bridge delegate() contract. The orchestrator routes `secondary:codex` coding tasks here; the primary calls the bridge and Codex does the work in its own loop.

name: Gemini runtime
summary: Runtime Faculty (secondary) — drives `gemini -p` behind the uniform RuntimeAdapter + runtime-bridge delegate() contract. The orchestrator's image/video roles resolve to `secondary:gemini`; the primary calls the bridge and receives the artifact path(s).

name: Scheduler
summary: Cron-style job scheduler. Machine-global jobs file (~/.garrison/scheduler-jobs.json); add/remove/list/run-now plus tick and daemon modes. Fitting setup hooks register jobs; the io.garrison.scheduler launchd agent runs the daemon.

name: Vault Git Sync
summary: Commit + push the Basic Memory Obsidian vault to git on a schedule (default nightly), so memory written by any agent syncs across machines. Pure shell; registered as a scheduler job.
